items.title: Iran War Splits Asia’s Diesel Market Into Haves and Have-Nots
items.desc: The war in the Middle East has left Asian buyers grappling with a two-speed diesel economy, as poorer states face acute shortfalls, while wealthier nations with large refining industries enjoy sizable buffers.

items.title: Cairn says build cost inflation hits 2.5% due to Iran war
items.desc: Housebuilder Cairn Homes said that while not weakening customer sentiment, the ongoing geopolitical volatility has impacted build cost inflation, which is now running at about 2.5%.

items.title: Glencore’s Traders Score Big Profits as War Rattles Energy Markets
items.desc: Glencore Plc’s trading team made bumper profits as the Iran war roiled global commodity markets, with surging prices helping to put the firm’s marketing unit on course for one of its best-ever results.

items.desc: Revenue climbed 7% to 155.99 billion rupees, supported by its mainstay home-care business - home to brands such as Comfort and Vim - whose revenue increased 9%.

items.title: How rising jet fuel prices are driving up the cost of fighting wildfires
items.desc: The Iran war has nearly doubled jet fuel prices in the United States. That means the bill for firefighting aircraft operations this summer will likely rise by tens of millions of dollars.
